problem.title: Search a 2D Matrix
problem.description: Write an efficient algorithm that searches for a value in an m x n matrix. This matrix has the following properties: Integers in each row are sorted from left to right. The first integer of each row is greater than the last integer of the previous row.
assumptions: Rows sorted, first element of each row > last element of previous row.

example.explanation: Rows are sorted, first element of each row > last of previous. Treat as flat sorted array: binary search on row then column.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search
solutions.description: Treat the m×n matrix as a single sorted array of length m×n and apply binary search using index arithmetic to convert a 1D mid-point back to 2D coordinates (row = mid / n, col = mid % n). Because the rows are sorted and each row starts after the previous ends, the entire matrix is globally sorted, making this O(log(mn)) binary search valid. The key insight is the index mapping that lets us avoid flattening the matrix.
